subtitle: Marie on leave all week - critical handover and DTP1092 comments pending
context: Marie Cooksey is on annual leave 8-13 June and will return Monday 15 June. Her absence blocks approval of DTP1092 College Staff in PXD Proposal comments (she has left multiple unresolved comments requiring response), volunteer reporting queries for the Pay & Conditions programme, H&S Management System shortlist feedback, and DSE/Cardinus data verification. James Salas Guillen is on annual leave from today through Thursday 18 June, affecting H&S handover items, Cority uploads to production, and PeopleXD contractor account creation requests - Kevin and Chris are covering the H&S support queue and OSM escalations. Three unread emails from Marie (sent 4 June) on volunteer reporting, H&S system evaluation, and DTP1092 comments require your immediate attention as action items cannot wait for her return. Marie's comments on DTP1092 (received 14:16, 14:45, 4 June) and follow-up replies (8:53, 5 June) are the most time-critical blocker this week. Open Marie's 'Question re Volunteer reporting' email first - it contains the Pay & Conditions programme request that Julie Hickman partially answered but needs your coordination.

urgent.title: DTP1092 - College Staff in PXD Proposal - unresolved comments
urgent.sub: Marie's comments require response while she is away. Multiple comment threads from 4 June (14:16, 14:45) and 5 June (8:53) on the college data proposal are pending. Kevin replied 17:51 on 4 June but Marie's follow-up (8:53, 5 June) indicates further clarification needed. Cannot proceed without addressing her input.
urgent.subject: Marie Cooksey left a comment in "DTP1092 - College Staff in PXD Proposal"

urgent.title: Volunteer reporting - Pay & Conditions programme response
urgent.sub: Marie needs coordination between Julie Hickman's answer and your approval. Marie requested confirmation from Julie Hickman on volunteer reporting capabilities for Pay & Conditions. Julie replied (10:07, 4 June) stating only annual leave and sick leave can be self-requested. Marie will need your sign-off on the response to send to Pay & Conditions team.
urgent.subject: Question re Volunteer reporting

urgent.title: H&S Handover - review outstanding items
urgent.sub: James Salas Guillen is on leave until 18 June. Handover document (15:44, 5 June) outlines current outstanding H&S items. Kevin and Chris are covering - you must review and assign immediate priorities, particularly Cority production upload and any escalations.
urgent.subject: H&S Handover

needs.title: Cority production upload - signed SR and next steps
needs.sub: James Salas Guillen sent signed SR for Cority test-to-production migration (11:49, 4 June). Production start date needs confirmation. James is on leave until 18 June - may need to action or delegate to Sharon/Rob per his handover.
needs.subject: RE: Cority upload to the test server - next steps to production server

needs.title: H&S Management System - shortlist and weighting feedback
needs.sub: Marie sent guidance (16:05, 4 June) requesting team input on Strategic H&S Modular System requirements evaluation weightings over the next few weeks. Helen Harbour scheduled a diary item (13:52, 5 June) to review proposed weightings. Coordinate with Helen on timeline and Kevin's input while Marie is away.
needs.subject: H&S Management System

needs.title: DSE/Cardinus data interface verification
needs.sub: James Salas Guillen confirmed (10:58, 5 June) automated PeopleXD-Cardinus upload is live. Marie asked for confirmation on manual contractor record creation (11:36, 5 June) and James replied (15:15, 5 June) H&S admin can access. Ensure process is fully documented and monitored in James's absence.
needs.subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

fyi.sub: Simon Burford has suggested Daphne contact you on HALO data work (17:34, 4 June). Scope unknown - awaiting contact.
